brief.decisionQuestion: Should Shipkit add a reproducible public search baseline before the external pilot?
assumptions: GitHub repository search is a bounded first discovery provider.
brief.evidenceThreshold: Every material claim requires an exact verified quote.
protectedOutcomes: Keep the six-person pilot thresholds unchanged

questions: Can ranked public discovery be reconstructed?
What limitation could invalidate the recommendation?
stopConditions: Support and falsification evidence are captured and reviewed.

queries.rationale: Find the primary Shipkit repository result.
Search explicitly for a limitation or contrary implementation.

selections.conflictOfInterest: The repository describes its own implementation.

claims.statement: Shipkit has a bounded search and exact-citation implementation path.
claims.uncertainty: The fixture proves the baseline contract, not external product value.

supportingCitations.quote: "description":"Shipkit uses bounded search and exact citations."

claims.statement: Repository search is not equivalent to general web discovery.
claims.uncertainty: A later provider can expand beyond repositories.

supportingCitations.quote: "description":"Repository search alone is not a general web index."

opportunities.title: Add one reproducible search provider
opportunities.problem: Explicit URL manifests cannot discover omitted public repositories.
opportunities.expectedOutcome: Queries, ranks, result digests, selected sources, and citations are reconstructible.
opportunities.uncertainty: Repository search is narrower than web search.
opportunities.learningValue: Tests whether discovered evidence improves pilot decisions.
opportunities.smallestExperiment: Run one support and one falsification query.

opportunities.title: Stay explicit-manifest only
opportunities.problem: Search results can change between runs.
opportunities.expectedOutcome: Maximum input control at the cost of discovery coverage.
opportunities.uncertainty: Missed sources may dominate the decision.
opportunities.smallestExperiment: Compare one manifest run with one search run.

opportunities.title: Add a general web provider now
opportunities.problem: Repository search does not cover the wider web.
opportunities.uncertainty: Cost, safety, and ranking quality are unproven.
opportunities.smallestExperiment: Do not start before the repository baseline is measured.

decision.rationale: One provider is the smallest falsifiable step before the six-person pilot.

experiment.hypothesis: A ranked support and falsification search improves evidence coverage without changing pilot thresholds.
experiment.method: Run the bounded provider, capture selected sources, and verify exact quotes.
rollbackPlan: Remove the provider adapter and retain evidence for review

acceptanceCriteria: Support and falsification queries pass independent review
verificationPlan: Run deterministic provider and source fixtures
